name: ECS Architecture
description: Entity-Component-System architecture for games: entities are IDs, components are data bags, systems are pure functions that query and mutate components. Enables data-oriented design for performance and scalability.

requiredElements.name: entity-manager
requiredElements.description: Entity creation/destruction with unique IDs and component registration.

requiredElements.name: component-store
requiredElements.description: Component storage as typed arrays or Maps for cache-friendly access.

requiredElements.name: system-runner
requiredElements.description: Systems run in order, each querying entities with required components.

requiredElements.name: query-system
requiredElements.description: Query API to find entities matching a component signature.

optionalElements.name: event-bus
optionalElements.description: Event system for cross-system communication (damage, death, spawn).

optionalElements.name: archetype-grouping
optionalElements.description: Archetype-based grouping for batch system iteration.

qualityRules.description: Data (components) and logic (systems) are cleanly separated.
Query API is typed and efficient — no O(n) filtering per system.
Systems are independent and composable — adding a feature = add a system.
Component storage is cache-friendly; no per-entity object allocation in loops.
Entity destruction disposes 3D resources tied to components.
Systems are pure functions testable in isolation with mock data.
New components/systems added without modifying existing code.

antiPatterns.description: Single component with 20 fields mixing transform/health/render — defeats ECS purpose.
Systems directly reference each other — tight coupling, hard to test.
Creating new objects per entity per frame in systems — GC pressure.
Destroyed entities leave GPU resources undisposed — leak.

improvementSuggestions.description: Group entities by component signature for O(1) system iteration.
Pool entities to avoid GC on frequent create/destroy.
Add event bus for decoupled cross-system communication.

exampleOutputs.label: ECS system query pattern
